sentence: She go to school every day.
correct: She goes to school every day.
explanation: Third person singular (she) requires <goes>, not <go>.

correct: He doesn't like vegetables.
He does not like vegetables.
explanation: Third person singular (he) requires <doesn't>, not <don't>.

sentence: The children plays in the park.
correct: The children play in the park.
explanation: <Children> is plural, so the verb should be <play> without <-s>.

correct: My friends are very nice.
explanation: <Friends> is plural, so use <are> instead of <is>.

correct: The dog barks at night.
explanation: Third person singular (the dog) requires <barks> with <-s>.

correct: She has two brothers.
explanation: Third person singular (she) requires <has>, not <have>.

sentence: They goes to the gym on Mondays.
correct: They go to the gym on Mondays.
explanation: <They> is plural, so the verb should be <go> without <-s>.

sentence: The cat sleep on the sofa.
correct: The cat sleeps on the sofa.
explanation: Third person singular (the cat) requires <sleeps> with <-s>.

sentence: My mother cook dinner every evening.
correct: My mother cooks dinner every evening.
explanation: Third person singular (my mother) requires <cooks> with <-s>.

correct: We don't understand the question.
We do not understand the question.
explanation: <We> is plural, so use <don't>, not <doesn't>.

sentence: The birds sings in the morning.
correct: The birds sing in the morning.
explanation: <Birds> is plural, so the verb should be <sing> without <-s>.

correct: He watches TV after dinner.
explanation: Third person singular (he) requires <watches> with <-es>.

sentence: The teacher explain the lesson clearly.
correct: The teacher explains the lesson clearly.
explanation: Third person singular (the teacher) requires <explains> with <-s>.

sentence: My parents works in a hospital.
correct: My parents work in a hospital.
explanation: <Parents> is plural, so the verb should be <work> without <-s>.

correct: She doesn't know the answer.
She does not know the answer.
explanation: Third person singular (she) requires <doesn't>, not <don't>.

correct: The baby cries every night.
explanation: Third person singular (the baby) requires <cries> — change <y> to <ies>.

sentence: Tom and Jerry is best friends.
correct: Tom and Jerry are best friends.
explanation: Two subjects joined by <and> are plural, so use <are>.

sentence: There is many books on the table.
correct: There are many books on the table.
explanation: <Books> is plural, so use <there are>, not <there is>.

correct: The water is cold.
explanation: <Water> is an uncountable noun (singular), so use <is>.

correct: Everybody knows the answer.
explanation: <Everybody> is singular, so the verb needs <-s>: <knows>.

sentence: The news are on TV at 8 pm.
correct: The news is on TV at 8 pm.
explanation: <News> looks plural but is actually singular, so use <is>.

sentence: My sister and I goes to the same school.
correct: My sister and I go to the same school.
explanation: Two subjects joined by <and> are plural, so use <go>.

correct: Each student has a textbook.
explanation: <Each> is always singular, so use <has>.

correct: The information is very useful.
explanation: <Information> is an uncountable noun (singular), so use <is>.

correct: Nobody likes homework.
explanation: <Nobody> is singular, so the verb needs <-s>: <likes>.
